review_notes_addressed.shared_critical_finding: Both reviewers flagged route shadowing: financeRouter mounted at /api had legacy /payments and /expenses handlers that intercepted the new paymentsRouter/expensesRouter, making the entire Sprint 3 write path unreachable. Root cause deeper than initially reported — the `rolesGuard(['SUPER_ADMIN'])` wrapper on the financeRouter mount was blocking ALL /api/* traffic including the new routers (preventing TECHNICIAN from creating REPAIR expenses). Fixed in 2cf3c68 by removing the legacy handlers from finance.ts AND moving the SUPER_ADMIN guard to per-route inside financeRouter via a `superAdminOnly` helper, with no wrapper on the mount.
product_fixes: #1 (CRITICAL): route shadowing resolved — paymentsRouter + expensesRouter reachable; 6 breakage scenarios (A–F) closed
#2 (HIGH): top-5 debtors panel added to /finance — backed by topDebtors field in computeFinanceDashboard
#3 (MEDIUM): booking picker replaces raw CUID input in add-payment + add-expense modals (fetches GET /api/bookings?status=CONFIRMED,ISSUED&limit=100)
#4 (MEDIUM): ENUM English → Russian via PAYMENT_STATUS_LABELS map in debts page; /day TECHNICIAN hints translated
#5 (MEDIUM): donut caption 'Учтены только одобренные расходы' added
#6 (MEDIUM): React.Fragment key replaces bare fragment in debts .map()
technical_fixes: C1+H1+H2: route shadowing resolved (removed legacy handlers + per-route guards)
H3: HTTP-level integration tests added — payments.routes.test.ts (5 cases) + expenses.routes.test.ts (7 cases) covering 401/403/400/201/409/TECHNICIAN-REPAIR-only

M1: date-range boundaries switched from inclusive `lte: endOfMonth` to half-open `lt: startOfNextMonth` across computeMonthlyTrend / computeFinanceDashboard / computePaymentsCalendar / computeExpensesBreakdown
L1: Decimal import unified — paymentService, expenseService, finance.ts all import from `decimal.js`
L2: dynamic `await import('../prisma')` replaced with top-level import in routes/payments.ts + routes/expenses.ts
L4: contradictory `bookingId!` + `if (bookingId)` resolved by dropping the `!`
deferred_non_blocker: Technical M2: pre-existing Number() coercion in /profit legacy code (not introduced by Sprint 3)
Technical M3: computeDebts in-memory filter (fine at current scale)
Technical M4: paymentStatusSyncForAllBookings per-request overhead (pre-existing; Sprint 4 follow-up)
Technical M5: schema verification of Expense.date vs expenseDate (schema confirmed — no new column; no bug)
Technical L3/L5: cosmetic (approvedOnly coercion, string negative Decimal) — deferred
Technical minor: dead `endOfMonth` helper in finance.ts — safe to remove in follow-up
Product #5: donut approved-only filter — documented on page (explicit toggle deferred)
